description: Build a Docker image from Dockerfile
explanation: Builds a Docker image from the Dockerfile in the current directory and tags it with the given name.

description: Run a Docker container with port mapping
explanation: Starts a container from the image and maps a host port to a container port.

description: Run a container in detached mode with a name
explanation: Starts a container in the background with a custom name for easy reference.
Lists all currently running Docker containers. Use -a to include stopped ones.
Lists all containers on the system, both running and stopped.
Gracefully stops the specified running container.
Removes a stopped container from the system.
Removes the specified Docker image from the local image store.

description: Open an interactive shell in a running container
explanation: Opens an interactive bash shell inside a running container for debugging.
Streams the log output of a container in real-time.
Builds, creates, and starts all services defined in docker-compose.yml in the background.

description: Stop and remove all containers and networks
explanation: Stops and removes all containers, networks, and optionally volumes from a compose project.
Removes all unused images, containers, networks, and optionally volumes to reclaim disk space.
Installs all dependencies listed in package.json into the local node_modules directory.
Installs the specified package and adds it to the dependencies in package.json.

description: Install a package as a dev dependency
explanation: Installs the package and adds it to devDependencies, used for development-only tools.
Runs the dev script defined in package.json, typically starting a dev server.
Runs the build script defined in package.json, typically creating a production build.
Runs the test script defined in package.json.
Scaffolds a new Next.js project with the latest version in the specified directory.
Installs the specified Python package from PyPI.

description: Install all Python dependencies from requirements file
explanation: Installs all packages listed in the requirements.txt file.
Creates a new Python virtual environment in the venv directory for isolated dependencies.
Activates the virtual environment, making its Python and packages the default.

description: Export installed Python packages to requirements file
explanation: Outputs all currently installed pip packages with exact versions to a requirements file.
Creates a new Rust project with a standard directory structure and Cargo.toml.

description: Build a Rust project in release mode
explanation: Compiles the Rust project with optimizations enabled for production.
Initializes a new go.mod file, creating a new Go module.

description: Compile a Go program into a binary
explanation: Compiles the Go source code into an executable binary.
Executes the specified target defined in the Makefile.
Executes the specified JavaScript file using the Node.js runtime.

description: Type-check a TypeScript project without emitting files
explanation: Runs the TypeScript compiler in check-only mode to find type errors without producing output.
Lints the codebase and automatically fixes fixable linting errors.
Formats all supported files in place according to Prettier rules.

description: Show the last 5 commits in one-line format
explanation: Displays the 5 most recent commit messages, one per line.

description: Follow logs from all compose services
explanation: Streams log output from all services defined in the compose file in real-time.
Lists all Docker images stored locally on the system.
Lists all named Docker volumes on the system.
Lists all Docker networks configured on the system.
Runs Playwright browser-based end-to-end tests.

description: Find and delete all empty directories recursively
explanation: Recursively searches the current directory tree for empty directories and removes them. Useful for cleaning up project structures.

description: Delete log files older than 30 days
explanation: Finds files matching the given pattern modified more than 30 days ago and deletes them.
Searches for files exceeding the specified size. Helpful for tracking down disk space usage.
Recursively finds and removes all .tmp files in the current directory tree.
Locates files with world-writable permissions, which can be a security risk.

description: Find node_modules directories without descending into them
explanation: Locates node_modules directories while preventing find from descending into them, saving time.
Recursively sets read/write for owner and read-only for group/others on all regular files.
Recursively sets rwx for owner and rx for group/others on all directories.
Locates symbolic links that point to non-existent targets.

description: Find files newer than a reference file
explanation: Finds files modified more recently than the specified reference file.

description: List files in current directory only (not recursive)
explanation: Lists only regular files in the current directory without descending into subdirectories.
Locates all zero-byte files in the directory tree.
Searches for JPEG image files regardless of case (e.g., .jpg, .JPG, .jpeg).

description: Find files containing a specific text pattern
explanation: Searches within files for a text pattern and lists the filenames that contain it.

description: Safely search file contents with special characters in names
explanation: Uses null-terminated output to safely handle filenames with spaces or special characters.

description: Count lines in all Python files sorted by size
explanation: Counts lines in each Python file and sorts the results numerically.
Searches for files while excluding anything inside .git directories.
Removes all .bak backup files from the directory tree.

description: Find files owned by root user
explanation: Locates files owned by the specified user.

description: Find files owned by a specific group
explanation: Locates files belonging to the specified group.
Creates a directory structure including all parent directories as needed.
Copies an entire directory tree from source to destination.
Moves or renames a file from one path to another.

description: Force remove a directory and all contents
explanation: Recursively and forcefully deletes a directory and all its contents without prompting. Use with extreme caution.
Creates a symbolic link (shortcut) pointing to the target path.

description: Show total size of current directory
explanation: Displays the total disk usage of the current directory in human-readable format.

description: List directory contents sorted by size
explanation: Shows the size of each item in the current directory sorted from largest to smallest.

description: Show disk space usage for all filesystems
explanation: Displays available and used disk space on all mounted filesystems in human-readable format.
Displays a visual tree of the directory structure up to 2 levels deep, excluding node_modules.
Displays detailed metadata about a file including size, permissions, timestamps, and inode.

description: Create empty file or update timestamp
explanation: Creates a new empty file or updates the access and modification times of an existing file.
Adds execute permission to a file, allowing it to be run as a program.
Changes the owner and group of a file.
Creates a gzip-compressed tar archive of the specified directory.
Extracts the contents of a gzip-compressed tar archive into the current directory.
Creates a zip archive of the specified directory recursively.

description: Extract a zip archive to a specific directory
explanation: Extracts the contents of a zip archive into the specified destination directory.
Efficiently synchronizes files between source and destination, transferring only differences.
Creates a hard link that points to the same inode as the original file.

description: Delete files not accessed in 90 days
explanation: Removes files that have not been accessed in over 90 days.
Creates a new empty Git repository in the current directory or reinitializes an existing one.
Downloads a copy of a remote repository to your local machine.
Displays the current state of the working directory and staging area.
Stages all modified, new, and deleted files for the next commit.
Allows you to interactively choose which hunks of changes to stage.

description: Commit staged changes with a message
explanation: Records the staged changes to the repository with a descriptive message.

description: Add changes to the last commit without changing the message
explanation: Adds currently staged changes to the most recent commit, keeping its original message.

description: Push commits to remote main branch
explanation: Uploads local commits to the remote repository on the specified branch.

description: Push and set upstream for a new branch
explanation: Pushes a new branch to the remote and sets up tracking so future pushes only need "git push".

description: Fetch and merge from remote main branch
explanation: Fetches changes from the remote and merges them into the current branch.

description: Pull and rebase local commits on top of remote
explanation: Fetches remote changes and replays your local commits on top, avoiding merge commits.
Downloads objects and refs from all remotes without modifying your working directory.
Lists all local branches. Use -a to also show remote branches.
Creates a new branch but does not switch to it.

description: Create and switch to a new branch
explanation: Creates a new branch and immediately switches to it.
Modern command to switch between branches. Replaces git checkout for branch switching.

description: Merge a branch into the current branch
explanation: Integrates changes from the named branch into the current branch.
Replays your current branch commits on top of the main branch, creating a linear history.

description: Show commit history as a compact graph
explanation: Displays a compact visual representation of the commit history.

description: Show commits by a specific author
explanation: Filters the commit history to show only commits by the specified author.
Shows the differences between your working directory and the staging area.
Shows the differences between the staging area and the last commit.

description: Discard the last commit and its changes
explanation: Resets the current branch to one commit before HEAD, discarding all changes permanently.

description: Undo the last commit but keep changes staged
explanation: Moves HEAD back one commit but keeps all changes staged, ready to recommit.

description: Create a commit that undoes the last commit
explanation: Creates a new commit that reverses the changes of the last commit, preserving history.
Saves your uncommitted changes and reverts the working directory to a clean state.

description: Restore the most recently stashed changes
explanation: Reapplies the most recently stashed changes and removes them from the stash list.
Displays all saved stashes in the current repository.

description: Apply a specific commit to the current branch
explanation: Applies the changes from an existing commit onto the current branch.
Creates a tag pointing to the current commit, typically used for releases.
Displays all remote repositories configured for the current project with their URLs.
Adds a new remote repository reference to the current project.

description: Show a log of all reference changes
explanation: Shows a log of where HEAD has been, useful for recovering lost commits.

description: Begin binary search for a bug-introducing commit
example: git bisect start && git bisect bad HEAD && git bisect good v1.0.0
explanation: Begins a binary search through commit history to find which commit introduced a bug.

description: Show who last modified each line of a file
explanation: Displays the author and commit for each line of a file, useful for tracking down changes.

description: Stop tracking a file without deleting it
explanation: Removes a file from Git tracking but keeps it on disk.
Removes all untracked files and directories from the working tree.
Sets the default name that will be attached to all your commits across all repositories.
Sets the default email that will be attached to all your commits across all repositories.

description: Show commits from the last 2 weeks
explanation: Filters the commit history to show only commits made after the specified date.
Outputs the given text to the standard output.

description: Display the contents of a file
explanation: Reads and displays the entire contents of the specified file.
Concatenates the contents of multiple files and writes the result to a new file.

description: List all files including hidden ones in long format
explanation: Lists all files including hidden ones with detailed information like permissions, size, and date.
Lists files in human-readable format sorted from largest to smallest.

description: List files sorted by modification time
explanation: Lists files sorted by modification time, most recently modified first.
Changes the current working directory to the specified path.

description: Go back to the previous directory
explanation: Switches back to the previous working directory.
Displays the absolute path of the current working directory.

description: Find the path of an executable
explanation: Locates the full path of the specified executable in the PATH.

description: Find the binary, source, and manual for a command
explanation: Locates the binary, source, and manual page files for the specified command.

description: Show the manual for a command
explanation: Displays the manual page with detailed documentation for the specified command.

description: Show quick help for a command
explanation: Displays a brief help summary including available flags and options.

description: Show the last 20 commands from history
explanation: Displays the last 20 entries from your shell command history.
Creates a shortcut name for a longer command, valid for the current shell session.
Sets an environment variable available to the current shell and child processes.
Displays all currently set environment variables.

description: Show the current date in YYYY-MM-DD format
explanation: Displays the current date and time in the specified format.
Displays a formatted calendar for the current month.
Pauses execution for the specified number of seconds.

description: Measure how long a command takes to run
explanation: Executes the command and reports the elapsed wall-clock, user, and system time.
Runs the specified command repeatedly at 5-second intervals, showing output full-screen.
Examines the file and reports its type based on its content, not just the extension.

description: Extract the filename from a path
explanation: Strips directory and suffix from a path, returning just the filename.

description: Extract the directory from a path
explanation: Strips the filename from a path, returning just the directory portion.

description: Resolve a symbolic link to its absolute target
explanation: Follows all symlinks and prints the absolute path of the final target.

description: Calculate the MD5 hash of a file
explanation: Computes and prints the MD5 checksum of the specified file for integrity verification.

description: Calculate the SHA-256 hash of a file
explanation: Computes and prints the SHA-256 checksum of the specified file.
Encodes the input file or data into Base64 format.
Decodes Base64-encoded data back to its original binary form.

description: Display a hex dump of a file
explanation: Displays a hexadecimal dump of the file contents alongside the ASCII representation.

description: Pipe output of one command as input to another
explanation: Sends the standard output of the first command as input to the second command.
Writes the command output to a file, overwriting any existing content.
Appends the command output to the end of the specified file without overwriting.
Merges standard error into standard output so both can be piped or redirected together.
Redirects the contents of the file as standard input to the command.

description: Set file permissions to owner read/write only
explanation: Sets the file to be readable and writable only by the owner, a common setting for private keys.

description: Set permissions to owner rwx, others rx
explanation: Gives the owner full control while allowing others to read and execute but not write.
Sets the default permission mask that determines permissions for newly created files.

description: Re-run the last command with sudo
explanation: Re-executes the previous command with elevated privileges. Useful when you forgot sudo.

description: Get only the HTTP status code from a URL
explanation: Fetches a URL and prints only the HTTP response status code.

description: Send a POST request with data
explanation: Sends an HTTP POST request with form-encoded data.
Fetches only the HTTP response headers without the body.
Follows HTTP redirects (3xx responses) to the final destination.
Sends HTTP Basic Authentication credentials with the request.
Downloads a file from a URL without displaying progress output.
Resumes a previously interrupted download from where it left off.
Sends 4 ICMP echo requests to test network connectivity to a host.

description: Show listening ports and associated processes
explanation: Lists all TCP and UDP listening ports along with the processes using them.

description: Show listening ports (modern replacement for netstat)
explanation: Modern tool to display socket statistics, showing listening TCP/UDP ports and processes.

description: Find which process is using port 8080
explanation: Lists processes that have the specified port open.

description: Look up DNS records for a domain
explanation: Queries DNS servers to find the IP address and other DNS records for a domain.

description: Query DNS records for a domain
explanation: Performs DNS lookups with detailed output including query timing and server info.

description: Get just the IP address for a domain
explanation: Returns only the resolved IP address without verbose DNS output.

description: Trace the network path to a host
explanation: Displays the route and transit delays of packets across a network to a destination.

description: Connect to a remote server via SSH
explanation: Establishes a secure encrypted shell connection to a remote host.

description: Connect to SSH on a custom port
explanation: Connects to an SSH server listening on a non-default port.

description: Connect using a specific SSH key
explanation: Authenticates using a specific private key file instead of the default.

description: Copy a file to a remote server
explanation: Securely copies a file from the local machine to a remote server over SSH.

description: Copy a file from a remote server
explanation: Securely copies a file from a remote server to the local machine.

description: Copy a directory to a remote server
explanation: Securely copies an entire directory tree to a remote server.

description: Sync a directory to a remote server over SSH
explanation: Synchronizes a local directory to a remote server using SSH for transport.
Displays or configures network interface parameters and addresses.

description: Show IP addresses of all interfaces
explanation: Modern replacement for ifconfig, showing IP addresses and interface details.
Displays the kernel routing table showing how packets are routed.

description: Show all IP addresses of this machine
explanation: Displays all IP addresses associated with the host.
Measures and displays detailed timing information for each phase of an HTTP request.

description: Check if a port is open on a remote host
explanation: Tests whether a TCP port is open on a remote host without sending data.

description: Request a URL with automatic decompression
explanation: Requests compressed responses and automatically decompresses them.

description: Generate a new ED25519 SSH key
explanation: Generates a new ED25519 SSH key pair, recommended for modern security.

description: Copy SSH public key to a remote server
explanation: Installs your public SSH key on a remote server for passwordless authentication.

description: Upload a file via multipart form POST
explanation: Uploads a file to a server using multipart form-data encoding.
Routes the HTTP request through the specified proxy server.

description: Capture HTTP traffic on a network interface
explanation: Captures and displays network packets on the specified interface and port.
Retrieves registration details for a domain name from WHOIS databases.

description: Fetch URL silently but show errors
explanation: Suppresses progress output but still displays error messages.
Enables the specified network interface.

description: Retry a failed request up to 3 times
explanation: Retries the request up to the specified number of times on transient errors.

description: Forward local port 8080 to remote port 80 via SSH tunnel
explanation: Creates an SSH tunnel forwarding a local port to a remote service.
Displays a snapshot of all running processes with detailed information including CPU and memory usage.
Filters the process list to show only processes matching the given name.
Displays a dynamic, real-time view of running system processes and resource usage.
An enhanced interactive process viewer with color output and easier navigation.

description: Force kill a process by PID
explanation: Sends SIGKILL to immediately terminate a process. The process cannot catch or ignore this signal.
Sends SIGTERM to request a graceful shutdown, allowing the process to clean up.
Sends a signal to all processes matching the given name.

description: Kill processes matching a full command line pattern
explanation: Kills processes whose full command line matches the given pattern.

description: Run a command immune to hangups in background
explanation: Runs a command in the background that continues running after you log out.

description: Run a command in the background
explanation: Runs the command in the background, returning the shell prompt immediately.
Lists all currently active background jobs in the current shell session.
Brings the specified background job to the foreground.

description: Resume a stopped job in the background
explanation: Resumes a suspended job and continues it in the background.

description: Run a command with lower priority
explanation: Runs a command with reduced scheduling priority, making it more polite to other processes.

description: Change priority of a running process
explanation: Adjusts the scheduling priority of an already running process.
Starts a virtual terminal session that persists even after disconnecting.
Creates a new named tmux session for persistent terminal management.

description: Attach to an existing tmux session
explanation: Reconnects to an existing detached tmux session.

description: Find processes by name with full command line
explanation: Lists PIDs and full command lines of processes matching the pattern.

description: Wait for all background jobs to finish
explanation: Blocks until all background jobs in the current shell have completed.

description: Show system uptime and load average
explanation: Displays how long the system has been running, number of users, and load averages.

description: Show memory usage in human-readable format
explanation: Displays total, used, and free memory in human-readable units.
Lists all signal names and numbers that can be sent to processes.

description: Send a hangup signal to reload configuration
explanation: Sends SIGHUP, commonly used to tell a process to reload its configuration.
Sends SIGSTOP to suspend a process. It can be resumed later with SIGCONT.
Sends SIGCONT to resume a previously stopped process.
Displays or sets the maximum number of open file descriptors for the shell.

description: Get the PID of a running process
explanation: Returns the process IDs of all running processes with the given name.
Repeatedly runs a command at the specified interval, showing the output full-screen.

description: Run a command with a time limit
explanation: Runs a command and kills it if it runs longer than the specified duration.

command: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
example: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
explanation: Refreshes the package index and upgrades all installed packages to their latest versions.
Installs the specified package and its dependencies from the configured repositories.

description: Remove a package but keep config files
explanation: Removes the specified package but leaves its configuration files in place.

description: Remove a package and its config files
explanation: Removes the package along with all its configuration files.
Removes packages that were automatically installed to satisfy dependencies and are no longer needed.
Installs the specified package on Red Hat-based distributions.
Installs the specified package using DNF, the modern Fedora package manager.
Starts the specified systemd service immediately.
Stops the specified systemd service immediately.
Stops and then starts the specified systemd service.

description: Enable a service to start on boot
explanation: Configures the service to start automatically when the system boots.

description: Disable a service from starting on boot
explanation: Prevents the service from starting automatically on boot.

description: Check the status of a service
explanation: Displays the current status, recent log entries, and running state of the service.
Tells systemd to reload its configuration after service unit files have been modified.
Displays all available system information including kernel name, version, and architecture.
Displays distribution-specific information including the distributor, release, and codename.
Displays the operating system identification information as defined by the OS.
Displays detailed CPU architecture information including cores, cache, and frequency.
Lists information about all available block devices (disks, partitions) in a tree format.

description: Mount a device to a directory
explanation: Attaches a filesystem on a block device to the specified mount point directory.
Detaches a previously mounted filesystem from the directory tree.
Lists the partition tables for all detected disk devices.
Immediately halts and powers off the system.
Immediately restarts the system.
Opens the crontab file for editing, allowing you to schedule recurring tasks.
Lists all scheduled cron jobs for the current user.
Permanently changes the system hostname, persisting across reboots.
Sets the system timezone to the specified region and city.

description: Create a new user with home directory
explanation: Creates a new user account with a home directory and bash as the default shell.

description: Delete a user and their home directory
explanation: Deletes the specified user account and removes their home directory.
Sets or changes the password for the specified user account.

description: Add a user to a group
explanation: Appends the user to the specified group without removing them from other groups.

description: Show groups a user belongs to
explanation: Lists all groups that the specified user is a member of.
Displays the user ID (UID) and group IDs (GID) for the specified user.
Displays all users currently logged into the system.
Displays the last 10 user login sessions recorded on the system.
Displays the last 20 kernel ring buffer messages, useful for hardware diagnostics.

description: Follow logs for a specific service
explanation: Streams the systemd journal logs for the specified service in real-time.

description: Show system logs from the last hour
explanation: Displays systemd journal entries from the specified time period.
Opens the sudoers configuration file for editing with syntax validation to prevent lockouts.

description: Search for a pattern recursively in all files
explanation: Recursively searches for a text pattern in all files, showing line numbers and filenames.
Searches for a pattern without regard to case.

description: Invert match: show lines NOT containing pattern
explanation: Displays all lines that do NOT match the given pattern.
Prints only the count of matching lines rather than the lines themselves.

description: Search for multiple patterns with extended regex
explanation: Uses extended regular expressions to match multiple alternative patterns.

description: Replace all occurrences of text in a file
explanation: Replaces every occurrence of "old" with "new" in the file content.

description: Replace text in-place in a file
explanation: Modifies the file in-place, replacing all occurrences of the pattern.

description: Print the first column of each line
explanation: Extracts and prints the first whitespace-delimited field from each line.

description: Print first column of a CSV file
explanation: Uses comma as the field separator and prints the first column from a CSV file.

description: Count and sort unique lines by frequency
explanation: Counts occurrences of each unique line and sorts by frequency, most common first.

description: Extract first field delimited by colon
explanation: Splits each line by the delimiter and extracts the specified fields.
Translates all lowercase characters to uppercase.
Deletes all newline characters, joining all lines into one.
Counts the number of lines in the specified file.
Counts the number of words in the specified file.

description: Show first 20 lines of a file
explanation: Displays the first N lines of a file.

description: Show last 20 lines of a file
explanation: Displays the last N lines of a file.

description: Follow a log file in real-time
explanation: Continuously displays new lines appended to a file in real-time.
Sorts the file and removes any duplicate lines.
Sorts lines numerically by the third colon-delimited field.
Removes consecutive duplicate lines. Use with sort to remove all duplicates.

description: Compare two files line by line
explanation: Shows the differences between two files line by line.
Recursively compares all files in two directories.
Merges lines from multiple files into columns separated by tabs.

description: Join lines from two files on a common field
explanation: Joins lines from two sorted files based on a common field.

description: Show lines common to two sorted files
explanation: Displays lines common to both sorted files.

description: Reverse each line of a file
explanation: Reverses the character order of each line.
Wraps each line to the specified width.
Reformats text paragraphs to fit within the specified width.

description: Number all lines in a file
explanation: Numbers all lines in the file, useful for referencing specific lines.
Formats comma-delimited data into a nicely aligned table.
Parses JSON and extracts the value at the specified key path.

description: Extract a field from all array elements
explanation: Iterates over a JSON array and extracts the specified field from each element.

description: List all keys in a JSON object
explanation: Lists all top-level keys in a JSON object.

description: Run a command for each line of input
explanation: Executes a command for each line of input, substituting the line in place of {}.

description: Write to a file and stdout simultaneously
explanation: Reads from stdin and writes to both stdout and one or more files.

description: Extract readable text from a binary file
explanation: Extracts sequences of printable characters from a binary file.
Uses Perl-compatible regular expressions for more powerful pattern matching.
Prints only even-numbered lines from the file.
Prints only the specified range of lines from the file.
